sarga.title: King Janaka honours Viswamitra, Rama and Lakshmana--narrates the story about the bow of Mahadeva--declares he would give Sita in marriage to Rama if he strings the bow.

shlokas.original:
तत: प्रभाते विमले कृतकर्मा नराधिप:।
विश्वामित्रं महात्मानं आजुहाव सराघवम्।।1.66.1।।
shlokas.translation: The day dawned bright and clear when the king (Janaka) performed his daily routine rites and welcomed the great Viswamitra with the two Raghavas (Rama and Lakshmana).

shlokas.original:
तमर्चयित्वा धर्मात्मा शास्त्रदृष्टेन कर्मणा।
राघवौ च महात्मानौ तदा वाक्यमुवाच ह।।1.66.2।।
shlokas.translation: The virtuous Janaka paid respects to Viswamitra and the highsouled Rama and Lakshmana in accordance with the sastras and spoke:

shlokas.original:
भगवन् स्वागतं तेऽस्तु किं करोमि तवानघ।
भवानाज्ञापयतु मामाज्ञाप्यो भवता ह्यहम्।।1.66.3।।
shlokas.translation: "Welcome, O Venerable one O Irreproachable one what can I do for you? Worthy of being commanded, I seek your orders.

shlokas.original:
एवमुक्तस्स धर्मात्मा जनकेन महात्मना।
प्रत्युवाच मुनिर्वीरं वाक्यं वाक्यविशारद:।।1.66.4।।
shlokas.translation: At the words of the magnanimous Janaka, the virtuous sage wellversed in speech replied to the heroic king:

shlokas.original:
पुत्रौ दशरथस्येमौ क्षत्रियौ लोकविश्रुतौ।
द्रुष्टुकामौ धनु श्श्रेष्ठं यदेतत्वयि तिष्ठति।।1.66.5।।
shlokas.translation: Dasaratha's sons, (Rama and Lakshmana), kshatriyas renowned in the world are eager to see that great bow in your possession.

shlokas.original:
एतद्दर्शय भद्रं ते कृतकामौ नृपात्मजौ।
दर्शनादस्य धनुषो यथेष्टं प्रतियास्यत:।।1.66.6।।
shlokas.translation: Be blessed. Show this great bow to the princes. This is their desire. Once it is fulfilled, they will go back.

shlokas.original:
एवमुक्तस्तु जनक: प्रत्युवाच महामुनिम्।
श्रूयतामस्य धनुषो यदर्थमिह तिष्ठति।।1.66.7।।
shlokas.translation: Thus addressed by the mighty ascetic Janaka replied, "I shall tell you how this bow came to be deposited here".

shlokas.original:
देवरात इति ख्यातो निमेष्षष्ठो महीपति:।
न्यासोऽयं तस्य भगवन् हस्ते दत्तो महात्मना।।1.66.8।।
shlokas.translation: "O Adorable one there was a famous king named Devarata, sixth in line from Nimi. This bow was kept with him in trust by the exalted lord (Shiva).

shlokas.original:
दक्षयज्ञवधे पूर्वं धनुरायम्य वीर्यवान्।
रुद्रस्तु त्रिदशान् रो��ात्सलीलमिदमब्रवीत्।।1.66.9।।
shlokas.translation: Earlier at the time of destruction of Daksha's sacrifice, Rudra endowed with  great  prowess lifted this bow sportively and spoke to the gods.

shlokas.original:
यस्माद्भागार्थिनो भागान्नाकल्पयत मे सुरा:।
वराङ्गाणि महार्हाणि धनुषा शातयामि व:।।1.66.10।।
shlokas.translation: 'O gods in your anxiety to partake your share of the sacrifie, you have failed to provide mine in the sacrificial offerings. Therefore, I shall sever your jewelled heads and beautiful limbs with this bow'.

shlokas.original:
ततो विमनसस्सर्वे देवा वै मुनिपुङ्गव।
प्रसादयन्ति देवेशं तेषां प्रीतोऽभवद्भव:।।1.66.11।।
shlokas.translation: "O Eminent ascetic thereafter all the gods with agitated minds propitiated lord of the gods, Siva and he was pleased with them.

shlokas.original:
प्रीतियुक्तस्स सर्वेषां ददौ तेषां महात्मनाम्।
तदेतद्देवदेवस्य धनूरत्नं महात्मन:। 
न्यासभूतं तदा न्यस्तमस्माकं पूर्व के विभो।।1.66.12।।
shlokas.translation: O Lord that gem of a bow belonging to Siva was given to the great gods who in turn got it deposited in trust with our ancestor.

shlokas.original:
अथ मे कृषत: क्षेत्रं लाङ्गूलादुत्थिता मया।
क्षेत्रं शोधयता लब्धा नाम्ना सीतेति विश्रुता।।1.66.13।।
shlokas.translation: Thereupon while I was ploughing and cleaing the (sacrificial) ground, Sita, a wellknown name, was lifted up by the blade of the plough. Thus she was obtained by  me.

shlokas.original:
भूतलादुत्थिता सा तु व्यवर्धत ममात्मजा।
वीर्यशुल्केति मे कन्या स्थापितेयमयोनिजा।।1.66.14।।
shlokas.translation: Arisen from the earth and not from a mother's womb, she grew up as my daughter. I made a stipulation that (this shall be the means to win this maiden as a gift) this shall be given in marriage only to the prince whose prowess is fully tried.

shlokas.original:
भूतलादुत्थितां तां तु वर्धमानां ममात्मजाम्।
वरयामासुरागम्य राजानो मुनिपुंगव।।1.66.15।।
shlokas.translation: O Eminent ascetic arisen from the earth and reared as my daughter, she has been sought after in marriage by many princes.

shlokas.original:
तेषां वरयतां कन्यां सर्वेषां पृथिवीक्षिताम्।
वीर्यशुल्केति भगवन् न ददामि सुतामहम्।।1.66.16।।
shlokas.translation: O Worshipful one all those monarchs sought this maiden but I did not offer my daughter saying that the 'marriage offer' could be made only through the tried and tested prowess of a suitor.

shlokas.original:
ततस्सर्वे नृपतय स्समेत्य मुनिपुंगव।
मिथिलामभ्युपागम्य वीर्यजिज्ञासवस्तदा।।1.66.17।।
shlokas.translation: O Preeminent among ascetics thereafter many kings together came to Mithila to test their prowess.

shlokas.original:
तेषां जिज्ञासमानानां वीर्यं धनुरुपाहृतम्।
न शेकुर्ग्रहणे तस्य धनुषस्तोलनेऽपि वा।।1.66.18।।
shlokas.translation: The bow was brought and placed before those who were curious to test their strength  but none was able to grasp or lift the bow.

shlokas.original:
तेषां वीर्यवतां वीर्यमल्पं ज्ञात्वा महामुने ।
प्रत्याख्याता नृपतयस्तन्निबोध तपोधन।।1.66.19।।
shlokas.translation: O great ascetic I knew those mighty kings had little strength, so rejected them.

shlokas.original:
तत: परमकोपेन राजानो नृपपुङ्गव।
न्यरुंधन्मिथिलां सर्वे वीर्यसंदेहमागता:।।1.66.20।।
shlokas.translation: O Mighty ascetic, thereafter all the kings doubting their own strength in stringing the bow were inflamed with anger and laid siege on Mithila.

shlokas.original:
आत्मानमवधूतं ते विज्ञाय नृपपुङ्गवा:।
रोषेण महताऽऽविष्टा: पीडयन्मिथिलां पुरीम्।।1.66.21।।
shlokas.translation: Those eminent kings felt humiliated. Inflamed with anger, they tormented the city of Mithila.

shlokas.original:
ततस्संवत्सरे पूर्णे क्षयं यातानि सर्वश:।
साधनानि मुनिश्रेष्ठ ततोऽहं भृशदु:खित:।।1.66.22।।
shlokas.translation: O Best among ascetics thus one year passed. Everywhere in the city all the means of living were exhausted. I felt deeply sad over this situation.

shlokas.original:
ततो देवगणान् सर्वान् तपसाऽहं प्रसादयम्।
ददुश्च परमप्रीता श्चतुरङ्गबलं सुरा:।।1.66.23।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, I propitiated the gods by my austerities. Highly pleased, they gave me an army of four divisions (chariots, elephants, horses and infantry ).

shlokas.original:
ततो भग्ना नृपतयो हन्यमाना दिशो ययु:।
अवीर्या वीर्यसन्दिग्धा स्सामात्या: पापकर्मण:।।1.66.24।।
shlokas.translation: Then those wicked kings who were exhausted were doubtful about their energy. They were beaten and defeated. They fled away along with their ministers in different directions.

shlokas.original:
तदेतन्मुनिशार्दूल धनु: परमभास्वरम्।
रामलक्ष्मणयोश्चापि दर्शयिष्यामि सुव्रत।।1.66.25।।
shlokas.translation: O Sage you are a tiger among asceties, you are faithful to your vows, I shall show this effulgent bow to your Rama and Lakshmana .

shlokas.original:
यद्यस्य धनुषो राम: कुर्यादारोपणं मुने।
सुतामयोनिजां सीतां दद्यां दाशरथेरहम्।।1.66.26।।
shlokas.translation: O Sage if Rama could lift and string this bow I shall give my daughter Sita, not born from a woman, to him (son of Dasaratha)".   इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे षट्षष्टितमस्सर्ग:।। Thus ends the sixtysixth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
